rationale: Previous asserts on `post1_proc_perm` / `post2_proc_perm` failed because the ensures-implication antecedent is not in scope for intermediate asserts (only `requires` is). The fix is to only emit facts derivable from `requires` and inject the `Seq::remove_value` / `no_duplicates` axioms via `seq_remove_lemma`, `seq_remove_lemma_2`, and `unique_implys_no_duplicates`, then plant a concrete trigger on `pre.children@.remove_value(child_proc_ptr@)` so z3 can align the two `post_k.children@ =~= pre.children@.remove_value(child_proc_ptr@)` ensures at the function-exit check.
